creator: ingeborg@fmgirl.com
text:
When trying to boot the FreeBSD installation disk, GRUB2 starts instead. I first burned the ISO to a memory stick, but when that failed several times I burned a DVD. It fails in exactly the same way.

There's a free partition on my disk, ready for an alternate system. The other system partition contains Linux Mint 17.3, if that makes a difference.
The computer is a fairly modern thing with i7 processor, 8GB RAM and a Xeon E3-1200 graphics card.

This is a very bad start for someone who wants to test the FreeBSD system. Does not seem to be worth the effort.
